summary.intent: Implement task #296: the ImageCropper agnostic core component for ars-ui, including the stateful machine, geometry math, keyboard handling, and test suite.
summary.outcome: Delivered the full ImageCropper agnostic core (Idle/Dragging/Resizing machine, 10-part ConnectApi, 64 tests, 19 snapshot fixtures, spec-conformance + proptest entries), reconciled all spec drift, passed a three-phase audit, and committed + pushed a PR closing #296.

repo: The `ComponentPart` derive emits a hand-written `Clone` (no `Copy`) because other `Part` enums in the workspace carry non-`Copy` fields (Key, SafeUrl, calendar dates). To opt a specific `Part` into `Copy`, add it explicitly: `#[derive(ComponentPart, Copy)]` — works whenever all variants/fields are `Copy`.
The current `Machine` trait requires `type Effect: EffectName` (a typed enum). The agnostic core cannot call `use_platform_effects()` directly; effects must be dispatched by the adapter.
`Bindable::get()` returns `&T`, not `T`. Mutating closures need explicit deref: `*ctx.crop.pending()`, not `ctx.crop.get()`.
`KeyboardKey` deliberately excludes printable characters. Matching '+'/'-' must use `data.character: Option<char>`, not `KeyboardKey::Character(...)`.
Multi-arg `MessageFn` fields trip clippy's `type_complexity` lint. The convention is a `pub type` alias (matches tabs/tree_view pattern).
Field-carrying `Part` variants (e.g. `Handle { position }`) require the field type to implement `Hash` for the `ComponentPart` derive to compile.
Snapshot count gate defaults: `min_per_variant=3`, `max_per_component=40`. 3 variants → need 9–40 snapshots minimum.

Controlled-mode sync pattern (on_change prop + SyncValue/SyncProps events + on_props_changed + effect type) is established in SignaturePad (#295) and must be replicated for every stateful component with a controlled prop.

ImageCropper does not need a rasterizer trait (unlike SignaturePad) because the core never owns source pixels — only a `src` URL string. The agnostic core's full contribution to export is `CropResult` + `CropOutputFormat`; pixel work is adapter-owned.

code.finding: SignatureRasterizer::rasterize takes core-owned vector stroke data. The trait exists because the core possesses the source. ImageCropper never possesses pixels, so no analogous trait is warranted.
CropHandle::token() arms for TopRight/BottomLeft/Top/Bottom/Left were initially untested — discovered by coverage analysis during the audit phase.

workflow: Always verify spec conventions against a recent reference implementation (SignaturePad was used here) before coding. Specs can silently lag behind engine API changes.
Run `cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject` with `--features i18n`; without it, i18n-gated snapshots in other crates trigger false orphan failures.

The `post-implementation-audit` skill is mandatory for this project and covers three phases: spec/impl drift, structural completeness (2 clean rounds required), and branch coverage. Run it before committing.
`cargo xci-fast` is the pre-push gate (~10–15 min); run it in the background while preparing the commit.
clippy skips `#[automatically_derived]` spans from proc macros, so `#[derive(ComponentPart, Copy)]` stays clean — `expl_impl_clone_on_copy` is not triggered.

friction: Spec §1.5 was written against an outdated engine: string-named effects with `use_platform_effects()` in the core, `Bindable::get()` returning `T` (not `&T`), and `KeyboardKey::Character('+')` (non-existent variant). All three caused compile failures and required reconciliation before any code could be written.
An API socket connection closed mid-session during the spec-sync phase, requiring a manual 'Continue' from the user.

`cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject` filtered to only image_cropper tests but flagged other crates' i18n-gated snapshots as orphans — required a second run with `--features i18n` and no test filter.
`.map(|value| value)` in a coverage test triggered clippy's `map_identity` lint and required a follow-up fix.
A rustdoc warning at the `handle_label` MessageFn type alias was only caught during the final verification pass, after the main implementation was already written.
Coverage gaps required four separate rounds of test additions (handle token arms → resize arms → dispatcher/part_attrs → keydown arms) before reaching an acceptable floor.
open_items: Adapter-side pixel export (using CropResult + CropOutputFormat to drive canvas/ctx.drawImage on web, and a decoded-buffer path natively) is explicitly out of scope for #296 and belongs in future leptos/dioxus adapter PRs.

`CropOutputFormat` currently has no in-core consumer beyond a unit test — it exists as shared vocabulary for adapters (same role as RasterFormat/RasterSpec in ars-core). No action needed until an adapter PR lands.
Codex-review loop was queued as the next step after PR creation but is not shown as completed in the transcript.

`Part` has non-focusable variants (Overlay, Image, Grid, Handle) that are now expressible in `Focus { part: Part::Overlay }` without a compile error, even though they're semantically meaningless. A bespoke focusable-only enum would close this, but was deliberately traded off against DRY.
